item.title: Fortnite: Dark Voyage Story Event Date & Time
item.description:
The end of Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 1 is now closer than ever. Players have less than a week to wrap up any unfinished business this season, including completing the Battle Pass and completing any last-minute quests. The current storyline continues to move forward, and whatever is to come next season is already being set up.

This article tells players what they need to know about the upcoming story event in Fortnite , so they can make sure that they're online before it begins and secure a front-row seat.

Fortnite Story Moment Release Date & Time

When Is the New Fortnite Story Moment?

The next story moment in Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 1 will take place on Saturday, March 14, at 3:30 PM ET . Epic Games has confirmed the date and time of the upcoming story moment, and fans are excited to see what the Dark Voyager intends to do next.

According to the official announcement post, the "Dark Voyager is on a tear across reality." He has targeted the sprite realm, and Lethal Labs is his next target. Players who want to witness the story moment on Saturday have to head to Lethal Labs a few minutes prior to 3:30 PM ET, as the event is scheduled to start then.

Fortnite story-event coverage — subscribe for expert insight

Subscribe to the newsletter for focused coverage of Fortnite story moments — narrative breakdowns, in-depth context on season shifts, and analysis of character arcs like the Dark Voyager — ideal for fans tracking the game's evolving narrative.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

Gamers will be immune to damage during the event, so that they aren't eliminated and don't have to worry about enemy fire while watching the experience.

Fortnite

item.title: Dungeons and Dragons Meets KPop Demon Hunters In This Homebrew Expansion That Is Blowing Up On Kickstarter
item.description:
Idols of the Neon Dark is a third-party Dungeons and Dragons supplement on Kickstarter that features the brand-new Idol class, nine subclasses, and K-Pop and Korean-themed monsters, spells, feats, magic items, and more. This homebrew Dungeons and Dragons supplement lets players of all ages bring the magic of KPop Demon Hunters to the TTRPG table.

Though Dungeons and Dragons has been around for over 50 years, it has seen a surge in popularity over the last decade, thanks to actual plays like Critical Role and appearances on TV shows like Stranger Things . Meanwhile, in summer 2025, KPop Demon Hunters took the world by storm, quickly becoming a global sensation and one of the top movies of the year.

Idols of the Neon Dark Brings KPop Demon Hunters to D&D

It was only a matter of time before two popular phenomena met – and now, they have in the form of a project on Kickstarter. Idols of the Neon Dark is a new third-party Dungeons and Dragons supplement, designed by a small team led by Dan Thut, that brings the bright fantasy of KPop Demon Hunters to the famous TTRPG. This supplement contains over 250 pages of K-Pop D&D content spread over four books, including tons of kid-friendly adventures and guidelines for younger fans.

  • Brand-new Idol class with five subclasses
    • Cadence of Aleatory
    • Cadence of Ceremony
    • Cadence of Discord
    • Cadence of Manifestation
    • Cadence of Percussion

  • Nine K-Pop-themed subclasses for existing official classes, including:
    • College of Collaboration Bard
    • Polyphony Domain Cleric
    • Harmonious Blade Fighter

  • Two playable species
    • Luminal
    • Fractalis

  • Over 30 monsters inspired by Korean folklore, including:
    • Dokkaebi
    • Sora, the Last Diva
    • Pink Crystal Dragon

  • More than 30 new Dungeons and Dragons spells, feats, and magic items
  • The K-Pop-inspired Lumenica setting
    • 80+ page adventure
    • Over 20 kid-friendly one-shots
    • More than 30 plot hooks
    • Over 30 NPCs for use in the Neon City

Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.

Your Order

Results

—

—

—

More Games
Rearrange the covers into the correct US release order.
Easy (5)Medium (7)Hard (10)

Your Order

Results

—
— —
More Games

Front and center in Idols of the Neon Dark is the Idol, a brand-new Dungeons and Dragons class with 20 levels of progression. The Idol does not use Spellcasting or Bardic Inspiration like a D&D Bard, and instead focuses on timing, reactions, and teamwork via Harmonies. Idols are further differentiated through their five subclasses. The Manifestation Idol summons a spirit companion, the Discord Idol punishes enemies for breaking Harmony, and the Percussion Idol helps keep rhythm until the beat drops. Alternatively, the Ceremony Idol uses rituals and repetition to enhance its allies, while the luck-based Aleatory Idol can even grant allies an extra turn if fortune favors them.

In addition to this class, nine new subclasses for the core classes in Dungeons and Dragons are coming, each inspired by the harmonious teamwork of the Idol. The College of Collaboration Bard is the leader of an ensemble, turning Bardic Inspiration into shared momentum to aid all of their allies at once. Meanwhile, the Polyphony Domain Cleric uses rhythmic, layered blessings to sustain their party. Fighters can instead choose the Harmonious Blade, forming a bond with a signature weapon that resonates and grows stronger with their performance.

Lumenica, a gorgeous high-magic Dungeons and Dragons setting centered around the K-Pop metropolis of Neon City, is the backdrop of over 100 pages of adventures, one-shots, plot hooks, and NPCs. Two new playable species can be found there: the Luminal, beings of living light and emotion, and the Fractalis, crystalline humanoids that refract magic. Beyond that, more than 30 monsters inspired by Korean folklore also appear in this book, including the elemental Dokkaebi tricksters, the Pink Crystal Dragon, and Sora, the Last Diva – a Kumiho who was one of the first Idols in Lumenica.

With over $112,000 pledged at press time, Idols of the Neon Dark has smashed its $13,000 goal nearly 10 times over. Indeed, this project was fully funded only four minutes after it launched.

Several stretch goals have already been unlocked, including a bonus battlemap, a Neon City wallpaper pack, 20 extra tokens for its supported virtual tabletops, and backer cameos as citizens of Lumenica. However, even more goals remain unmet, including rules and illustrations for a bowling minigame, a crowd energy system reminiscent of the Honmoon from KPop Demon Hunters , a two-player Duet mode, the Dance Idol subclass, and more. Idols of the Neon Dark is open for backing on Kickstarter until April 9, so fans still have plenty of time to support it, with fulfillment expected to take place in early 2027.

item.title: How to Get the Headset & Goggles in Paradox Junction (Black Ops 7 Zombies)
item.description:
The Headset in Paradox Junction can be tricky for players to obtain. Most players in Black Ops 7 Zombies might not know that the reason the Headset isn't spawning at its set locations is that they do not have Death Perception. The Headset only spawns if you're looking in the areas with Death Perception .

Below, we'll show you the exact visual areas where the Headset can spawn in Paradox Junction, along with how and where you can get the Goggles. Both of these items are needed for the Paradox Junction main easter egg, and we'll show you what they're used for below.

How to Get the Headset in Paradox Junction

  1. The Headset can only be obtained in Destroyed Nuketown once you have Death Perception .
  2. If Death Perception is not in your game due to the randomized Perk Machines, then you will need to acquire it from Der Wunderfizz or T.E.D.D. Trials. Der Wunderfizz is found in Destroyed Nuketown's Trinity Avenue after you have purchased 4 Perks.

Once you have Death Perception, you can find the Headset at one of the three locations we've listed below. To actually get the Headset, you must aim your gun close to the area we showcase in the images, where you'll be able to see a red outline of the Headset , then interact with it to pick it up.

  • In the Cul-De-Sac , inside the trash can by the southwest fence that leads to the Green House.
  • In the Green House Upstairs , hanging off the hat rack in the bedroom, when facing the northwest wall.
  • In the Yellow House Backyard , being held by the mannequin's right hand by the Perk Machine when facing east at the fence.

The Headset is invisible, and you can only pick it up when you see the outline of the Headset with Death Perception. If you can't see the outline, then adjust your aim or look at the other three locations. There are no additional spawns outside of the three we've listed.

How to Get the Goggles in Paradox Junction

  1. The Goggles can only be obtained in Destroyed Nuketown once you have Wisp Tea .
  2. If Wisp Tea is not in your game due to the randomized Perk Marchines, then you will need to purchase 4 Perks and then purchase Wisp Tea from Der Wunderfizz, which spawns on Trinity Avenue in Destroyed Nuketown.

With Wisp Tea, head to the south end of Trinity Avenue . To the left of Pack-a-Punch, just over the gate, is a wooden pole with speakers attached to the top. With any weapon, shoot the top of the speaker tower (usually under the top light).

This will cause the Goggles to drop from the pole, but they will remain out of bounds. To grab them, wait around Trinity Avenue and kill a zombie to make Wisp Tea activate. Wisp Tea will automatically rush to the Goggles and drag them out of bounds so you can pick them up .

It doesn't matter what Augments you have on Wisp Tea, as this step can be completed with any variation of the Perk.

How to Use the Goggles & Headset in Paradox Junction

  1. Now that you have the Goggles & Headset, you can enter the portal to return to Clean Nuketown.
  2. Inside Clean Nuketown, head south towards Trinity Avenue.
  3. By wooden boxes, and just before the Exfil Station, you can find a Toy Box.
  4. Interact with the Toy Box to place the Goggles and Headset inside.
  5. This is part of the main easter egg and will allow you to continue the next step, which involves searching for a ball in Destroyed Nuketown (which you can find here).

On the way to the final boss in Paradox Junction? Check out what loadouts we would recommend to make your fight easier! Or, if you're in the mood to hunt some collectibles, we've got a guide on all Zombie Intel you can find in Paradox Junction.

Call of Duty: Black Ops 7

item.title: Pokemon Legends: Z-A Home Support Gets an Update
item.description:
This article was updated by Andrea Trama on March 12, 2026, to reflect the new Z-A x Home promotions and how transferring Pokemon will work. It was first published on February 19.

Pokemon Legends Z-A will officially support Pokemon HOME connectivity in 2026, and now Pokemon fans can get more exciting updates. This highly anticipated feature was confirmed earlier last year, and a new tweet from Pokerapidinfo reveals that Pokemon Legends Z-A players will be able to transfer their critters into Pokemon HOME and from there into Pokemon Champions , the upcoming Pokemon title expected to launch later this year.

While the exact date for the launch of Pokemon Legends Z-A 's Pokemon HOME compatibility is still pending, the feature is expected to be detailed further this year after the news on Pokemon Day 2026 (February 27), aligning with the franchise's 30th anniversary celebrations. For now, one can speculate about the implications this feature could have for Pokemon Champions , a game that is now poised to support nearly every Pokemon from the franchise's history.

Pokemon Legends Z-A Confirms Compatibility with Pokemon HOME and Pokemon Champions

Pokemon Legends Z-A will support Pokemon HOME in 2026, allowing players to transfer their pocket monsters into Pokemon HOME and bring them back into the game at a later date. Pokemon Legends Z-A players can already link their save to HOME for some rewards, and even more down the line. Pokemon Legends: Z-A players can get free Alpha starters when connecting their save file to Pokemon HOME , with each being level 5. Not only that, but the game will be linkable to Pokemon Champions .

Drag weapons to fill the grid

Loadout Logic

Tap on a weapon to rotate it or press R while dragging.

Time —
Filled 0/36

Results

0
—
More Games
Drag weapons to fill the grid
EasyMediumHard

Loadout Logic

Tap on a weapon to rotate it or press R while dragging.
Time —
Filled 0/36
Results
0
—
More Games

The connectivity between Pokemon Legends Z-A and Pokemon HOME, allowing players to transfer critters into Pokemon Champions , is significant because it confirms that the battling game will likely feature compatibility with nearly every Pokemon in existence, from all generations. The game is expected to support:

  • All Pokemon in Pokemon Legends Z-A , or at least most of them, including any new additions introduced through its DLC, Mega Dimension.
  • Returning Mega Evolutions from past Pokemon titles, including the new ones featured in Pokemon Legends Z-A .
  • Pokemon from previous games that were previously unavailable or restricted in recent entries.

Pokemon's official website mentions a future promotion where you will be able to transfer a Pokemon Legends: Z-A Chesnaught, Delphox, Greninja, and Eternal Flower Floette to Pokemon Champions to get their exclusive Mega Stones in the game. Furthermore, Legends: Z-A is now confirmed to support two-way transfers with HOME, unlike what was previously reported in the leaks.

Key Details about Pokemon Legends Z-A Compatibility

  • Pokemon Legends Z-A to Pokemon HOME : Pokemon Legends Z-A will be fully compatible with Pokemon HOME , allowing players to move Pokemon in and out of the game.
  • Mega Evolutions : New Mega Evolutions introduced in Pokemon Legends Z-A and its DLC, Mega Dimension, will be supported in Pokemon Champions .
  • Pokemon Champions Compatibility : Pokemon Champions ' Pokedex may support most, if not all, Pokemon available across the Pokemon series, offering players an expansive experience with over 1,000 Pokemon species.
  • Mega Dimension DLC : Released on December 10, 2025, Mega Dimension introduced new storylines, new Mega Evolutions, and additional content for Pokemon Legends Z-A . These new critters will be transferable to Pokemon HOME and, by extension, usable in Pokemon Champions .

The fact that Pokemon Legends Z-A will be compatible with Pokemon HOME is a game-changer because it makes the game more flexible and provides an easier way to integrate it into the larger Pokemon ecosystem, especially with the upcoming Pokemon Champions . For fans, this also opens up exciting possibilities:

  • Players can move their favorite Pokemon into Pokemon Legends Z-A and then transfer them back into future Pokemon games, ensuring that their collection remains relevant across multiple games. It's unclear where Z-A mons will be transferable, though, and it remains to be seen whether Pokemon Gen 10 games will include this option.
  • The reintroduction of Mega Evolutions in Pokemon Legends Z-A and their transfer to Pokemon Champions is another major draw for players. This popular mechanic adds a level of strategy to battles, and it would be the first time the new Megas can be used in a turn-based game. It also means that the new Megas won't be a one-off gimmick, as they will soon be usable in a different game.

With Pokemon Legends Z-A confirming compatibility with Pokemon HOME and future transfers to Pokemon Champions , the Pokemon community has a lot to look forward to. Fans who are eagerly awaiting further details may finally get an actual date on Pokemon Day 2026, or at least a short roadmap about what's in store for all the critters caught in Z-A .

Pokemon Legends: Z-A

item.title: How to Get Squishy Clay in Pokopia
item.description:
Players have beautify the various towns in Pokemon Pokopia however they see fit, but since there are so many places for them to decorate, they will most likely want to experiment using all available construction materials, such as clay, a material that is necessary to make bricks.

This guide tells players how they can get their hands on Squishy Clay in Pokopia , and also how they can turn it into bricks once they've obtained it.

How to Get Squishy Clay in Pokopia

Players will most likely come across Squishy Clay for the first time after making it rain and freeing Onix from the cave in Withered Wasteland. Even though the cave near Onix has a decent amount of clay, they will eventually deplete it of its resources. This isn't a reliable way to find Squishy Clay, especially if players are planning on building several brick structures and will burn through their stock rather quickly.

The best way to farm Squishy Clay in Pokopia is to interact with a Pikachu doll and visit a Dream Island . The Pikachu doll Dream Island has several resources from the beach/ocean biome, including Squishy Clay. There is a layer of Squishy Clay on the lower part of the cliffs of this island, so players can farm several stacks of clay by using Rollout or Rock Smash.

How to Make Bricks in Pokopia

Once players have Squishy Clay, they can ask a Pokemon with the Burn ability to turn it into Brick. To do this, they have to talk to them, ask them to take a look at something, select the Squishy Clay in their inventory, and then choose how much clay they want to hand over.

Players will receive 2 Bricks for every piece of Squishy Clay they hand over. They can give each Pokemon 10 Squishy Clay at a time. So, if they want more than 20 Bricks, they have to give several Pokemon Squishy Clay.

Pokemon Pokopia
